text: What is the closed-cup flash point of non-volatile petroleum liquids?

options.text: 113 Deg F (45 Deg C) and above
140 Deg F (60 Deg C) and above

explanation: Non-volatile petroleum liquids are defined by their higher flash points. According to international safety standards for tanker operations, a liquid with a closed-cup flash point of 60°C (140°F) or above is considered non-volatile, reducing the risk of vapor accumulation at ambient temperatures.
